zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicolas de Condé於大約1171年出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德孔代-素-利斯考特時，他的父親Roger de CONDÉ43歲，母親Alix de MONS29歲。他於大約1210年與Elisabeth de Morialmé結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1230年在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯享年61歲，埋葬在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德孔代-素-利斯考特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicolas de Condé于大约1171年出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德孔代-素-利斯考特时，他的父亲Roger de CONDÉ43岁，母亲Alix de MONS29岁。他于大约1210年与Elisabeth de Morialmé结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1230年在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德瓦倫希恩斯享年61岁，埋葬在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德孔代-素-利斯考特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Condé
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicolas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nicolao
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

en.lifeSummary: When Nicolas de Condé was born about 1171, in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Roger de CONDÉ, was 43 and his mother, Alix de MONS, was 29. He married Elisabeth de Morialmé about 1210. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died about 1230, in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, at the age of 61, and was buried in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicolas de Condé est né environ 1171, à Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Roger de CONDÉ, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Alix de MONS, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Elisabeth de Morialmé environ 1210. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé environ 1230, à Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicolas de Condé um 1171 in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger de CONDÉ 43 und seine Mutter Alix de MONS 29. Er hat um 1210 Elisabeth de Morialmé geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb um 1230 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich und wurde in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas de Condé è nato il circa 1171 nel Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Roger de CONDÉ, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Alix de MONS, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Elisabeth de Morialmé circa 1210. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì circa 1230, nel Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicolas de Condéが約1171年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードコンドe-スル-ル'エスカウトで生まれた時，父Roger de CONDÉは43歳，母Alix de MONSは29歳であった。約1210年にElisabeth de Morialméと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は約1230年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードベレンシエンスで61歳で死去し，フランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードコンドe-スル-ル'エスカウトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicolas de Condé(이)가 대략 1171년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 콘드e-수-'스카트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de CONDÉ(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Alix de MONS(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1210년 Elisabeth de Morialmé(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1230년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 발시네스에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 콘드e-수-'스카트에 묻혔다.

mg.Abt 1171–Abt 1230: tany ho any amin'ny 1171–tany ho any amin'ny 1230

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas de Condé nasceu aproximadamente 1171, em Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Roger de CONDÉ, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Alix de MONS, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Elisabeth de Morialmé aproximadamente 1210. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1230, em Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicolas de Condé родился около 1171 г. в местности кондe-сур-л'ескаут, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Roger de CONDÉ было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Alix de MONS, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Elisabeth de Morialmé около 1210 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер около 1230 г. в местности веиленсиеннесь, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности кондe-сур-л'ескаут, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция.

sm.Abt 1171–Abt 1230: e tusa o le 1171–e tusa o le 1230

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicolas de Condé nació aproximadamente 1171, en Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Roger de CONDÉ, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Alix de MONS, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Elisabeth de Morialmé aproximadamente 1210. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1230, en Valenciennes, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.
